content.full_text:
JOHN S. COOPER
JUDGE OF THE SUPERIOR COURT
(RETIRED)
21 STEPHENS WAY
BERKELEY, CALIFORNIA 94705

January 7, 1991


Legal Division
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, CA  95804-0807

Re: Statement of Economic Interests

Ladies and Gentlemen:

     A few days ago, the office of the Alameda County Clerk
advised me that my name appeared on a list furnished them of
persons who might be required to file a Statement of
Interests for 1990. That office referred me to Lynn Martin
of the FPPC, to whom I indicated that it did not appear that
it was required to file a Form 721 at this time. She asked
that I write to you.

     I served on the Oakland-Piedmont Municipal court from
February 1, 1968, to December 29, 1970, and on the Superior
Court for Alameda County from that date until my retirement
on July 15, 1988. I timely filed all Annual Statements
required of me during that time, including the statement for
1987, filed on February 23, 1988. After retiring on July 15,
1988, I filed my Leaving Office Statement on July 18, 1988.

     As a retired judge, I served on assignment by the
Chairperson of the Judicial Council for the balance of July
and for the months of August through November, 1988. I
filed a Form 721 covering that period, on March 1, 1989.

     In 1989, I served on the Alameda County Superior Court
for approximately two months on assignment by the
Chairperson of the Judicial Council. I filed Form 721, by
reason of that service, on February 26, 1990.

     I did not serve on assignment at any time in 1990.
I have filed Statements of Economic Interests for all
periods in which I did serve. Accordingly it does not
appear that I am required to file a Form 721 at this time.

     Thank you for your assistance.

Very truly yours,

John S. Cooper

sections.question_synthetic: A retired judge seeks clarification on whether he is required to file a 1990 Statement of Economic Interests (Form 727), given that he did not serve on judicial assignment during that year.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is an incoming request letter from a private citizen (retired judge) to the FPPC, not an FPPC response. The document contains no formal QUESTION or CONCLUSION sections. The letter is a business correspondence requesting guidance on filing requirements. No FPPC advice or response is included in this document.

embedding.summary: Retired Judge John S. Cooper writes to the FPPC Legal Division to request clarification regarding his filing obligations for a Statement of Economic Interests for 1990, arguing that because he did not serve on judicial assignment that year, he should not be required to file a Form 727.
